rationale: The cited paper appears plausibly substantive because it studies a situational mechanism (moral licensing via self-concept) affecting luxury/indulgent vs necessity choices and donation behavior, which overlaps with the citing paper’s focal outcomes (hedonic vs utilitarian choices and charitable donation). The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior situational reason/feeling-related work to stable individual differences, so this 2006 licensing study could be a theory/comparison source rather than a mere incidental mention.
